Caveats: (a) the column is 94.7% one state, so naive one-hot leaves three very sparse measured levels (>200 1.46%, >300 1.24%) — monitor for overfitting / unstable coefficients; (b) under the R5 discharge filter (expired/hospice 2,423 rows dropped, 99,343 remain) the measured subset shifts slightly toward survivors, so recompute per-level positive_rates on the post-filter frame before trusting the exact lift.
alternatives_considered: Dropping the column because 94.7% is missing — rejected: the not-measured state and monotonic severity signal are informative (MNAR), and R3 forbids discarding the missingness fact.
Imputing NaN as 'Norm' — rejected, directly violates R3.
Collapsing >200 and >300 into a single 'High' band to fight sparsity — viable fallback, but loses the clean >300 lift (0.1432); kept separate unless instability forces a merge.

columns.semantic_meaning: Result of a hemoglobin A1c test (a ~3-month average glycemic-control marker) ordered during the encounter, banded as Norm (in-range), >7 (elevated), and >8 (poorly controlled). A NaN means the A1c was not ordered for that stay, NOT that control was normal. Available at the discharge decision point.
columns.value_interpretation: Heavily missing but less so than glucose: 84,748 of 101,766 rows (83.277%) missing, so 16.723% measured (verified per file). Among measured rows: >8 8,216 (8.073%), Norm 4,990 (4.903%), >7 3,812 (3.746%) — >8 (poor control) is the most common measured band, consistent with A1c being ordered when poor control is suspected. n_unique=3, is_constant=false. Critically, all three measured positive_rates sit BELOW the 0.1116 base rate and are nearly flat: Norm 0.09659, >8 0.09871, >7 0.10047 (all exact per file). The file's target_lift=0.9 is (most-extreme measured band)/base_rate (each measured band/base rounds to ~0.9), i.e. measured rows are uniformly ~0.9x the base rate; it is NOT an inverted within-measured spread. The signal is in measured-vs-not, not in the band value.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: Like glucose, whether an A1c is drawn depends on the clinician's (unobserved) judgment about glycemic management for that admission, so observation depends on the patient's underlying state — MNAR, codified by R3 (NaN = not ordered, not Norm). The co-missingness matrix (verified) shows A1Cresult-missing is essentially decorrelated from every other column's missingness (race -0.007, weight -0.028, payer_code -0.009, medical_specialty 0.018, diag_3 -0.037) and only weakly anti-correlated with max_glu_serum-missing (-0.07). That near-zero coupling rules out a shared administrative/data-export cause (which would show strong positive co-missingness with payer_code/medical_specialty), leaving a column-specific clinical-ordering mechanism — MNAR, not MCAR or globally-MAR. 83.3% missing rules out MCAR.
columns.target_relationship: Predictive content is almost entirely in the act of measurement, pointing the OPPOSITE way to glucose: measured rows have positive_rate 0.09848 (1,676/17,018) vs not-measured 0.11423 (9,681/84,748) — a derived -1.6pp difference, so 'A1c was checked' is weakly associated with LOWER 30-day readmission, plausibly because active glycemic management correlates with more deliberate diabetes care and discharge planning. Within the measured rows the bands barely separate (Norm 0.09659 vs >7 0.10047 vs >8 0.09871) and are slightly non-monotonic; each measured band is ~0.9x base (the file's lift 0.9). Net: keep the measured-vs-not contrast as the useful signal; expect the >7/>8 levels to contribute little on their own.
columns.recommended_strategy: Load with na_values='?' (R1). Do NOT impute and do NOT map NaN to 'Norm' (R3). Fill NaN with an explicit literal level 'NotMeasured' so the not-ordered state is its own category, yielding {NotMeasured, Norm, >7, >8}. Because the within-measured bands are nearly flat against the target, also emit a binary A1c_measured indicator (1 if Norm/>7/>8, else 0) — this single flag captures most of the usable (and verified, -1.6pp) signal and is robust. Keep the three measured bands for the tree model but expect them low-importance; never let '?' leak in as a category.
columns.imputation_rationale: Imputation is unjustified: MNAR + informative missingness (R3) means any fill toward Norm or mode would assert good glycemic control for 83% of encounters and erase the measured-vs-not signal, which is the only part that moves the target. Making missingness explicit (its own level) preserves both that signal and the weak band information without inventing values.
columns.modeling_transform: One-hot encode {NotMeasured, Norm, >7, >8}; for the LR baseline rely primarily on the A1c_measured binary indicator since the bands are near-collinear with the base rate. Optionally an ordinal Norm<>7<>8 (NotMeasured off-axis as a separate flag) for tree models, but given lift 0.9 / flat bands the ordinal direction is not informative, so the binary measured flag is the priority encoding.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No R4 leakage — the A1c is drawn and resulted during the stay, known at discharge. Caveats: (a) the below-base-rate measured bands mean a naive reader could wrongly conclude 'high A1c lowers readmission'; the honest reading is that the band value is near-null and the signal is measured-vs-not (measured 0.0985 < not-measured 0.1142) — document this in the feature log to avoid a misleading SHAP narrative. (b) Recompute per-level rates on the post-R5-filter frame (expired/hospice removed, 99,343 rows) before finalizing, since the filter can nudge these already-small differences.
alternatives_considered: Dropping A1Cresult because within-measured bands barely separate the target — rejected: the measured-vs-not contrast is informative (and below base rate), R3 forbids discarding the missingness signal; keep at least the A1c_measured flag.
Imputing NaN to 'Norm' — rejected, direct R3 violation.

Reducing the column to ONLY the binary measured indicator and dropping the bands — reasonable lightweight option given flat band rates; kept the bands for the tree model but flagged as expected low-importance rather than discarding.

columns.semantic_meaning: Whether first-line oral biguanide metformin was administered during the encounter and, if so, whether its dose changed. Four states: No (not on the drug this stay), Steady (on it, unchanged), Up (dose increased), Down (dose decreased). Recorded at discharge, reflects in-stay prescribing; known at the decision point.
columns.value_interpretation: Highest-coverage drug in the family and genuinely non-constant. No=80.359% (81,778), Steady=18.028% (18,346), Up=1.048% (1,067), Down=0.565% (575). 19,988 non-No rows (19.641%), 1,642 active dose changes (Up+Down). Verified against facts file.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing=0, pct_missing=0.0 in the facts file. This column is not '?'-coded: 'No' is an explicit intended level meaning the drug was not administered, so there is no NaN and no MCAR/MAR/MNAR question. R1/R3 do not apply (no missingness, not a lab-test column).
columns.target_relationship: Weak but real and statistically resolvable on the high-n levels. Per-level positive_rate: No=0.11517, Steady=0.09713, Up=0.08247, Down=0.12000 vs base 0.1116. The facts-file target_lift=1.075 is max(level_rate)/base (driven by Down=0.12), NOT a max/min-across-levels ratio. The actual max/min ratio is 1.455 (0.12/0.08247). The protective signal lives in Steady: its 95% CI (0.093, 0.101) excludes base, so on-metformin-Steady patients readmit genuinely less. Up/Down cells are thinner; treat their rates as noisier.
columns.recommended_strategy: Keep all four levels; do not collapse to a binary on/off — the Steady-vs-No gap (0.097 vs 0.115, CI-significant) carries the signal. One-hot the four states; ensure 'No' is its own level and is never coerced to NaN (there are no NaNs here, so na_values='?' has no effect on this column).
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation required: zero missing, and 'No' is a meaningful prescribing state, not a placeholder. Treating 'No' as a value rather than 'unknown' is the correct reading of the schema.
columns.modeling_transform: one-hot (4 levels); tree models can also take the raw 4-level encoding directly.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none — in-stay medication/dose decision is known at discharge (R4 safe). No discharge-filter interaction beyond the global R5 expired/hospice row drop.
alternatives_considered: Collapse to binary on-metformin flag (rejected: loses the CI-significant Steady<No protective gap)
Drop as low-signal (rejected: highest-coverage drug in family, Steady gap usable)

columns.semantic_meaning: Meglitinide (short-acting insulin secretagogue) administration / dose-change status during the encounter. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down, captured at discharge.
columns.value_interpretation: Rare drug. No=98.488% (100,227), Steady=1.360% (1,384), Up=0.108% (110), Down=0.044% (45). Only 1,539 non-No rows (1.512%) and 155 active dose changes. Up has 110 rows, Down 45 — both very thin. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' explicitly encodes not-administered. Not '?'-coded, so no missingness mechanism to classify. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Apparent but noise-dominated. positive_rate: No=0.11127, Steady=0.13150, Up=0.18182 (20/110), Down=0.06667 (3/45). The facts-file target_lift=1.629 is max(level_rate)/base, driven entirely by the 110-row Up cell whose 95% CI (0.110, 0.254) INCLUDES base — i.e. NOT statistically distinguishable. The Steady lift (0.1315 vs 0.111) is more stable but applies to only 1.36% of rows. Net: essentially no reliable standalone signal.
columns.recommended_strategy: Collapse directional levels: keep a binary on/off flag (No vs any-of {Steady,Up,Down}) plus optionally a single 'dose_changed' flag, rather than one-hotting Up/Down separately. Folding Up+Down (155 rows) prevents the model chasing the unstable, CI-non-significant 18% Up rate. Do not drop entirely — the Steady direction is plausible and the column feeds an aggregate med-burden count.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' is a real state. Rare-level collapsing, not imputation, is the relevant action.
columns.modeling_transform: bucket to binary on/off (+ optional changed flag); avoid one-hot on Up/Down to control variance.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (in-stay, R4 safe). Caveat: the high facts-file target_lift is a small-sample artifact (Up cell CI includes base), not a strong feature — do not over-weight it.
alternatives_considered: Keep 4-level one-hot (rejected: Up/Down cells too sparse, Up CI includes base)
Drop (borderline; kept for med-burden aggregate value)

columns.semantic_meaning: Meglitinide (rapid insulin secretagogue) administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge.
columns.value_interpretation: Very rare. No=99.309% (101,063), Steady=0.656% (668), Up=0.024% (24), Down=0.011% (11). Only 703 non-No rows; 35 active dose changes (Up=24, Down=11). Effectively a thin Steady-vs-No flag with negligible directional content. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level, not a NaN. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Essentially null. positive_rate: No=0.11158, Steady=0.11677, Up=0.04167 (1/24), Down=0.09091 (1/11). Facts-file target_lift=1.046 (=max level rate Steady/base). Steady barely differs from base (0.117 vs 0.112); Up/Down (1 positive each) are pure noise.
columns.recommended_strategy: Collapse to a single binary on/off flag (No vs not-No), discarding Up/Down direction (only 35 rows). If the cleaning pipeline prunes near-zero-variance med flags by a coverage threshold (e.g. <1% non-No), nateglinide (0.691% non-No) falls under it. Recommend: keep as a binary flag if a global on/off med-count feature is built; otherwise drop.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' meaningful. Action is variance-based pruning, not imputation.
columns.modeling_transform: bucket to binary on/off, or drop; never one-hot the 4 levels.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Caveat: near-zero variance — contributes almost nothing individually; value only inside an aggregate 'number of antidiabetic meds' feature.
alternatives_considered: Drop outright (defensible at 0.69% coverage)
4-level one-hot (rejected: Up/Down each <25 rows)

columns.semantic_meaning: First-generation sulfonylurea administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge. An older, rarely-used agent.
columns.value_interpretation: Near-constant. No=99.915% (101,680). Only 86 non-No rows: Steady=79 (0.078%), Up=6 (0.006%), Down=1 (0.001%). Effectively constant; the Up (6) and Down (1) cells are noise. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No NaN/'?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: None usable. positive_rate: No=0.11164, Steady=0.06329 (5/79), Up=0.0 (0/6), Down=0.0 (0/1). Facts-file target_lift=1.0 (No is the highest non-degenerate rate, so max-rate/base rounds to 1.0). With 86 non-No rows the per-level rates have no statistical power.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP the column. Effectively zero-variance (99.915% single value, only 86/101,766 deviating, 7 of those in the unusable Up/Down cells). Document the drop with count evidence (non-No=86, 0.085%). Optionally absorb into an aggregate 'rare-sulfonylurea-used' indicator; standalone it adds noise and dimensionality.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing. Drop is driven by lack of variance, not missingness.
columns.modeling_transform: drop (or fold into an aggregate med-count flag).
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Caveat: near-constant — keeping a one-hot risks an all-but-empty dummy that adds variance without signal.
alternatives_considered: Keep as binary flag (rejected: 0.085% coverage, no power)
Keep 4-level (rejected: Up/Down are 6 and 1 rows)

columns.semantic_meaning: Third-generation sulfonylurea administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge. A moderately common oral agent.
columns.value_interpretation: Usable coverage. No=94.899% (96,575), Steady=4.589% (4,670), Up=0.321% (327), Down=0.191% (194). 5,191 non-No rows (5.101%), 521 active dose changes. Steady has enough volume to model; Up/Down thinner but non-trivial. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Weak/mixed, concentrated in Steady. positive_rate: No=0.11211, Steady=0.10021, Up=0.11315, Down=0.12887 (25/194). Facts-file target_lift=1.155 (=Down rate/base). Steady is CI-significantly protective: its 95% CI (0.092, 0.109) excludes base. The Down=0.129 cell looks higher but its CI (0.082, 0.176) INCLUDES base — not a reliable Down signal, contrary to any directional reading.
columns.recommended_strategy: Keep with levels collapsed to {No, Steady, Up_or_Down}: Steady (4,670, CI-significant) stands alone; Up (327) and Down (194) are best merged into a single 'dose_changed' level to stabilize estimates since neither is individually CI-significant. One-hot the resulting 3 levels.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' meaningful. Action is rare-level merging, not imputation.
columns.modeling_transform: one-hot 3 levels (No / Steady / changed), or raw 4-level for tree models.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). No discharge-filter interaction beyond global R5 row drop.
alternatives_considered: Binary on/off (rejected: loses CI-significant Steady protective gap)
Full 4-level one-hot (acceptable for trees; merged for linear models because Up/Down CIs include base)

columns.semantic_meaning: First-generation sulfonylurea (obsolete) administration status. Only No/Steady observed here, so effectively a binary flag. Captured at discharge.
columns.value_interpretation: De-facto constant. No=99.999% (101,765), Steady=0.001% (exactly 1 row). Two distinct levels, one with a single observation. The most degenerate column in the family. The facts file sets is_constant=false because n_unique=2, but for modeling it is constant. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: None. positive_rate: No=0.11160 (=base), Steady=0.0 (0/1). Facts-file target_lift=1.0. A single Steady row carries zero statistical information.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP the column. Effectively zero-variance (one non-No row out of 101,766). Same disposition as the examide/citoglipton-style near-constant case: document drop with count evidence (Steady=1, 0.001%). No information loss.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing. Drop is mandated by zero modeling variance, not missingness.
columns.modeling_transform: drop.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Keeping it would create an all-zero-but-one dummy that can destabilize regularized linear models.
alternatives_considered: Keep as binary flag (rejected: 1 deviating row = constant)
Fold into med-count (negligible)

columns.semantic_meaning: Second-generation sulfonylurea administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge. One of the most-prescribed oral agents in the cohort.
columns.value_interpretation: High coverage, good variance. No=87.534% (89,080), Steady=11.159% (11,356), Up=0.757% (770), Down=0.550% (560). 12,686 non-No rows (12.466%), 1,330 active dose changes — second-best coverage after metformin. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Directionally interesting but only partly reliable. positive_rate: No=0.11119, Steady=0.11166, Up=0.12857, Down=0.15179 (85/560). Facts-file target_lift=1.36 (=Down rate/base). Steady ~ base. The Down cell IS the one credible directional signal in the family: its 95% CI (0.122, 0.182) EXCLUDES base, so a dose REDUCTION at discharge plausibly flags instability/elevated risk. However the Up cell (0.12857, n=770) has CI (0.105, 0.152) that INCLUDES base, so Up is NOT individually distinguishable — the prior claim that Up is 'individually informative and well-populated' is overstated.
columns.recommended_strategy: Keep at least three levels {No, Steady, Down} with Down preserved as its own level because its rate is CI-significantly above base. Up may be kept separate for tree models or merged with Steady/into a 'changed' bucket for linear models since it is not CI-distinct from base. Do NOT collapse Down away. One-hot accordingly.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' meaningful. Down retained because it is CI-significantly predictive, not for completeness alone.
columns.modeling_transform: one-hot — keep Down as its own level (CI-significant); Up can be its own level for trees or merged for linear models.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). The dose-Down -> higher-readmit pattern is a legitimate at-discharge signal, not leakage.
alternatives_considered: Binary on/off (rejected: discards the CI-significant Down signal)
Merge Up+Down (acceptable, but loses the cleaner Down-only signal; Up alone is not CI-distinct from base)

columns.semantic_meaning: Second-generation sulfonylurea administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge. Common oral agent.
columns.value_interpretation: Good coverage. No=89.535% (91,116), Steady=9.113% (9,274), Up=0.798% (812), Down=0.554% (564). 10,650 non-No rows (10.465%), 1,376 active dose changes — comparable coverage to glipizide. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Very weak / nearly flat. positive_rate: No=0.11222, Steady=0.10729, Up=0.10468, Down=0.09220 (52/564). Facts-file target_lift=1.006, the lowest among the well-populated drugs (computed as max-level-rate/base; here No itself is the highest rate, so lift rounds to ~1.0). The gradient runs OPPOSITE to glipizide: glyburide dose-Down readmits slightly LESS. Net: little standalone separating power despite decent coverage.
columns.recommended_strategy: Keep, but collapse to {No, Steady, changed}: with effectively flat separation the directional levels add dimensionality without reliable signal, so merge Up+Down into one 'changed' level and one-hot 3 levels. Retain rather than drop because coverage is high and it contributes to an aggregate med-burden feature.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' meaningful. Action is rare-level merging given the flat target relationship.
columns.modeling_transform: one-hot 3 levels (No / Steady / changed), or raw 4-level for trees.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Caveat: near-flat target relationship — low standalone value; mostly useful as part of polypharmacy count.
alternatives_considered: Keep 4-level (acceptable for trees)
Drop (rejected: high coverage, useful in med-count aggregate)

columns.semantic_meaning: First-generation sulfonylurea (obsolete) administration status. Only No/Steady observed, so a binary flag, captured at discharge.
columns.value_interpretation: Near-constant. No=99.977% (101,743), Steady=0.023% (23 rows). Two levels; the non-No level has just 23 observations. De-facto constant for modeling. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: None usable. positive_rate: No=0.11161 (=base), Steady=0.04348 (1/23). Facts-file target_lift=1.0. 23 rows give no statistical power.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP the column. Near-zero-variance (23 non-No rows, 0.023%). Same disposition as acetohexamide/chlorpropamide. Document drop with count evidence. Optionally absorb into an aggregate rare-med flag.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing. Drop driven by lack of variance.
columns.modeling_transform: drop (or fold into med-count aggregate).
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Near-constant dummy adds noise if retained.
alternatives_considered: Keep as binary flag (rejected: 23 rows, no power)

columns.semantic_meaning: Thiazolidinedione (insulin sensitizer) administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge. Moderately common.
columns.value_interpretation: Usable coverage. No=92.799% (94,438), Steady=6.855% (6,976), Up=0.230% (234), Down=0.116% (118). 7,328 non-No rows (7.201%), but only 352 active dose changes (Up/Down thin). Steady dominates the non-No mass. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Modest; the apparent direction is NOT statistically reliable. positive_rate: No=0.11206, Steady=0.10421, Up=0.12393 (29/234), Down=0.15254 (18/118). Facts-file target_lift=1.367 (=Down rate/base) but the Down cell's 95% CI (0.088, 0.217) INCLUDES base — so the 'direction-driven' framing is an artifact of an 118-row cell, not a real Down signal. Steady is only mildly protective (0.104 vs 0.112) and is the more trustworthy effect.
columns.recommended_strategy: Keep with Up+Down merged into a single 'changed' level: Steady (6,976) stands alone and is mildly protective; Up (234) and Down (118) are too thin and CI-non-significant to trust separately, so merge to stabilize. One-hot 3 levels {No, Steady, changed}.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' meaningful. Rare-level merging given thin Up/Down cells.
columns.modeling_transform: one-hot 3 levels (No / Steady / changed), or raw 4-level for trees.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Caveat: the facts-file lift is inflated by the 118-row Down cell whose CI includes base — treat the directional effect as not established.
alternatives_considered: 4-level one-hot (acceptable for trees)
Binary on/off (rejected: loses Steady protective gap)

columns.semantic_meaning: Thiazolidinedione (insulin sensitizer) administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge.
columns.value_interpretation: Usable coverage, flat signal. No=93.745% (95,401), Steady=5.994% (6,100), Up=0.175% (178), Down=0.085% (87). 6,365 non-No rows (6.255%), only 265 active dose changes. Steady dominates non-No mass; Up/Down very thin. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Nearly flat. positive_rate: No=0.11207, Steady=0.10508, Up=0.10674 (19/178), Down=0.05747 (5/87). Facts-file target_lift=1.004 (No is the highest rate, so lift rounds to ~1.0). Steady marginally protective; Down low but on only 87 rows (uninformative). Little standalone separation.
columns.recommended_strategy: Keep with Up+Down merged into 'changed' (265 rows combined); Steady (6,100) stands alone. One-hot 3 levels. Retain mainly for the med-burden aggregate since standalone separation is minimal.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' meaningful. Rare-level merging given thin/flat directional cells.
columns.modeling_transform: one-hot 3 levels (No / Steady / changed), or raw 4-level for trees.
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Caveat: flat target relationship — low standalone value.
alternatives_considered: Drop (borderline given flat lift, but 6.3% coverage argues to keep for aggregate)
4-level one-hot (acceptable for trees)

columns.semantic_meaning: Alpha-glucosidase inhibitor administration / dose-change status. Levels No/Steady/Up/Down at discharge. Uncommon agent.
columns.value_interpretation: Very rare, near-constant. No=99.697% (101,458), Steady=0.290% (295), Up=0.010% (10), Down=0.003% (3). Only 308 non-No rows (0.303%); just 13 active dose changes. Effectively a thin Steady flag with noise-only direction. Verified.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: pct_missing=0.0; 'No' is the explicit not-administered level. No '?' present. R1/R3 not implicated.
columns.target_relationship: Noise only. positive_rate: No=0.11166, Steady=0.08475 (25/295), Up=0.2 (2/10), Down=0.33333 (1/3). Facts-file target_lift=1.001 (No is the highest non-tiny rate; the eye-catching Up=0.2 and Down=0.333 are 2/10 and 1/3 small-sample artifacts, which is why facts-file lift is ~1.0, not 3x).
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP the column (or at most fold into an aggregate rare-med flag). Near-zero-variance: 308 non-No rows, 0.303% coverage, with directional cells of 10 and 3 that cannot be modeled. Document drop with count evidence. If a coverage threshold (<1% non-No) prunes rare meds, acarbose is removed by it.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation: zero missing, 'No' meaningful. Drop is driven by lack of variance, not missingness.
columns.modeling_transform: drop (or binary on/off folded into med-count aggregate).
columns.leakage_or_caveat: none for leakage (R4 safe). Caveat: the 20% and 33% Up/Down rates are tiny-cell artifacts (n=10, n=3) — do NOT let a tree split on them.
alternatives_considered: Binary on/off flag (defensible only inside a med-count aggregate)
4-level one-hot (rejected: Up=10, Down=3 rows)

columns.semantic_meaning: Alpha-glucosidase-inhibitor oral antidiabetic agent. Encodes dose/prescription status during the encounter: No = not prescribed, Steady = continued unchanged, Up = dose increased, Down = dose decreased. The status is fixed by discharge (medication reconciliation), so it is a legitimate point-of-discharge feature.
columns.value_interpretation: Near-constant. Per facts: No = 101728 (99.963%, positive_rate 0.1116), Steady = 31 (0.030%, pr 0.06452), Down = 5 (0.005%, pr 0.4), Up = 2 (0.002%, pr 0.0). Only 38 of 101766 rows (0.037%) are non-No. The non-No per-level positive rates rest on n = 31/5/2 and are pure sampling noise, not signal.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0 and pct_missing = 0.0 in the facts file; this column has no NaN/'?' values. Drug status is explicitly coded with 'No' (not prescribed) rather than left blank, so there is no missingness to classify and MCAR/MAR/MNAR is not applicable. 'No' is an informative real level, not a disguised missing. miglitol does not appear in the dataset-level co_missing list, confirming zero missingness.
columns.target_relationship: Effectively none. target_lift = 1.0; the dominant 'No' level sits exactly at the 0.1116 base rate. Non-No levels (n = 31/5/2) are far too small for their positive rates to be interpretable. The column cannot separate the 30-day readmit class on its own.
columns.recommended_strategy: Drop as a standalone one-hot (near-zero variance: 99.963% single value, only 38 non-No rows). PREFERRED: do not discard the information entirely — fold the Up/Down rows into a single engineered aggregate 'n_meds_changed' (and on/off can feed 'n_meds_on') computed consistently across ALL medication columns, so the few dose-change signals survive in a denser feature. Keep that aggregate, drop the per-drug column.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation required (0% missing). Handling is variance-driven, not missingness-driven: a column constant for 99.963% of rows adds dimensionality and overfitting risk under grouped CV (rare levels can land entirely in one fold), so it should not survive as its own one-hot. The aggregate count preserves the residual signal at far lower variance cost.
columns.modeling_transform: drop standalone; fold Up/Down into a shared engineered meds-changed count (do not one-hot individually)
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No post-discharge leakage (R4 clean): dose-change status is set at/by discharge medication reconciliation. No R5 (discharge-filter) interaction. Caveat: rare-level rates (Down = 0.4 on n = 5) are noise and must never be read as predictive.
alternatives_considered: Keep as one-hot No/Steady/Up/Down
Collapse to binary on_miglitol
Fold into shared meds-changed count (preferred)

columns.semantic_meaning: Thiazolidinedione antidiabetic agent (withdrawn from the US market in 2000 for hepatotoxicity, consistent with the 1999-2008 window). Encodes prescription/dose status during the encounter; known at discharge, so a valid point-of-discharge field.
columns.value_interpretation: Binary and essentially constant per facts: No = 101763 (99.997%, pr 0.1116), Steady = 3 (0.003%, pr 0.0). n_unique = 2. Only 3 rows in 101766 record this drug; no Up/Down levels exist.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0. No NaN; status explicitly coded. 'No' is an informative 'not prescribed' level, not a disguised missing. Not in the co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: None. target_lift = 1.0; 'No' pr = 0.1116 (= base rate). The 3 'Steady' rows have pr = 0.0, meaningless at n = 3. No discriminative power.
columns.recommended_strategy: Drop. With only 3 non-No rows it is a de facto constant carrying no learnable signal. 'Steady' is not a dose CHANGE, so it would not even contribute to an aggregate meds-changed count; it could at most contribute to an 'n_meds_on' count, but at n = 3 that contribution is negligible. Remove the column.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation (0% missing). Removal justified by near-zero variance (99.997% single value) and clinical irrelevance (drug withdrawn mid-window).
columns.modeling_transform: drop
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No leakage (R4 clean), no R5 interaction. Caveat: troglitazone's withdrawal ~2000 means its rare appearance is partly a calendar/date artifact rather than a stable clinical signal — a further reason not to rely on it.
alternatives_considered: Keep as binary flag
Drop (chosen)

columns.semantic_meaning: First-generation sulfonylurea antidiabetic agent. Encodes prescription/dose status (No/Steady/Up; no 'Down' observed) during the encounter. Known at discharge, valid feature.
columns.value_interpretation: Near-constant per facts: No = 101727 (99.962%, pr 0.11161), Steady = 38 (0.037%, pr 0.07895), Up = 1 (0.001%, pr 0.0). Only 39 non-No rows in 101766. Per-level positive rates rest on n = 38/1 and are noise.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0. Status explicitly coded with 'No'; no NaN. 'No' = not prescribed, informative, not missing. Not in co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: Negligible. target_lift = 1.0; 'No' pr = 0.11161 (= base rate). Steady (0.07895, n = 38) and Up (0.0, n = 1) are too sparse to interpret. No class separation.
columns.recommended_strategy: Drop as a standalone one-hot (near-zero variance: 99.962% 'No', only 39 non-No rows). Fold the single 'Up' row into the shared engineered 'n_meds_changed' count so it is not lost, rather than keeping a per-drug one-hot.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation (0% missing). Drop justified by variance: constant for 99.962% of rows, no fold-level signal, and rare levels risk landing entirely in one grouped-CV fold.
columns.modeling_transform: drop standalone; fold Up into shared meds-changed count
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No post-discharge leakage (R4 clean). No R5 interaction. Caveat: rare-level rates are noise; do not interpret.
alternatives_considered: One-hot No/Steady/Up
Binary on/off
Fold into shared meds-changed count (preferred)
Drop

columns.semantic_meaning: Listed as an antidiabetic medication column in the source schema, but in this dataset every encounter is coded 'No', so it carries no information about any actual prescription. Nominally a point-of-discharge medication-status field.
columns.value_interpretation: TRUE ZERO VARIANCE per facts: No = 101766 (100.0%), n_unique = 1, is_constant = true; facts note: 'Zero variance — single value. Drop before modelling.' NOTE: the facts file reports positives = 0 and positive_rate = 0.0 for the single 'No' level and target_lift = null. The positives = 0 figure is an ARTIFACT of how the constant column was profiled (all 11357 dataset positives necessarily fall in the only existing level); it must NOT be read as 'no positives have examide=No'. Either way a single-valued column has zero discriminative power.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0 and n_unique = 1. No NaN and no variation; missingness is not a concept that applies. The uniform 'No' is a coded 'not prescribed', not a disguised missing. Not in co_missing list.
columns.target_relationship: None by definition. A constant column has zero correlation with any target; target_lift is null in the facts precisely because there is only one level. It cannot separate any class.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP unconditionally before modelling — true zero-variance constant (101766/101766 = 'No', n_unique = 1, is_constant = true). It cannot even contribute to an aggregate meds-on/meds-changed count (every value is 'No'). Record the drop with count evidence in docs/FEATURE_LOG.md.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation possible or needed (0% missing). A constant contributes nothing, can break some scalers/encoders, and inflates dimensionality; the facts file note and standard practice both mandate dropping it.
columns.modeling_transform: drop
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No leakage (R4 clean), no R5 interaction. Caveat: a sklearn VarianceThreshold(0.0) or explicit constant-column guard in clean.py should catch examide automatically so it is never one-hot encoded.
alternatives_considered: Drop (only sensible option for a true constant)

columns.semantic_meaning: Listed as an antidiabetic medication column in the source schema, but like examide every encounter is coded 'No', so it conveys no prescription information. Nominally a point-of-discharge medication-status field.
columns.value_interpretation: TRUE ZERO VARIANCE per facts: No = 101766 (100.0%), n_unique = 1, is_constant = true; facts note: 'Zero variance — single value. Drop before modelling.' As with examide, the reported positives = 0 / positive_rate = 0.0 for the single level and target_lift = null is a profiling artifact of a constant column, not evidence of any relationship. Identical situation to examide.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0, n_unique = 1. No NaN, no variation; missingness does not apply. The uniform 'No' is a coded 'not prescribed', not a disguised missing. Not in co_missing list.
columns.target_relationship: None by definition. Constant column → zero discriminative power; target_lift is null. Cannot separate the readmit class.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP unconditionally before modelling (true zero-variance constant: 101766/101766 = 'No', n_unique = 1). Cannot contribute to any aggregate count (all 'No'). examide and citoglipton are the canonical zero-variance pair to drop; record both in docs/FEATURE_LOG.md with count evidence.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation possible or needed (0% missing). A constant adds zero information and real risk (encoder/scaler breakage, dimensionality); drop is mandatory per the facts note and standard preprocessing.
columns.modeling_transform: drop
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No leakage (R4 clean), no R5 interaction. Caveat: ensure a VarianceThreshold / constant-column guard removes both examide and citoglipton so neither leaks into the one-hot feature matrix.
alternatives_considered: Drop (only sensible option for a true constant)

columns.semantic_meaning: Insulin therapy status during the encounter — the single most clinically central diabetes medication. No = not on insulin, Steady = continued, Down = dose decreased, Up = dose increased. Up/Down reflect active glycemic-management adjustments by the clinical team, fixed at discharge, making this a high-value point-of-discharge feature.
columns.value_interpretation: The ONLY high-variance, well-populated column in this family, per facts: No = 47383 (46.561%, pr 0.10037), Steady = 30849 (30.314%, pr 0.11128), Down = 12218 (12.006%, pr 0.13898), Up = 11316 (11.120%, pr 0.1299). All four levels are densely populated — no sparsity problem. This is the clinically and statistically meaningful medication column of the group.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0. Insulin status is explicitly coded for every encounter; no NaN/'?'. 'No' means 'not on insulin', a genuine clinical state, not a disguised missing. Not in co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: Clear and clinically sensible gradient, target_lift = 1.245 (the strongest in this family). positive_rate: No = 0.10037 (below base rate — not on insulin = lower readmit risk), Steady = 0.11128 (≈ base rate), Up = 0.1299, Down = 0.13898 (highest). NOTE: the gradient is NOT strictly monotonic in dose direction — Down (0.139) exceeds Up (0.130); the discriminative axis is really 'active dose change vs none' plus a 'not-on-insulin = lower risk' contrast, plausibly because dose changes flag unstable/poorly-controlled glycemia. This is a real, retainable signal.
columns.recommended_strategy: KEEP. One-hot encode the four levels with 'No' as the reference/baseline. Additionally engineer a binary 'insulin_changed' = (Up OR Down) flag and let Up/Down feed the shared 'n_meds_changed' aggregate, since dose change is the discriminative axis. Do NOT collapse all four levels — both the No-vs-Steady (on/off) and the change-vs-Steady contrasts carry signal.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation needed (0% missing). Retention is justified by genuine variance (all four levels densely populated) and a clinically interpretable, materially-above-base-rate relationship with 30-day readmission for the dose-change levels (lift 1.245).
columns.modeling_transform: one-hot (No baseline) + engineered binary insulin_changed flag; optionally ordinal {not-on < Steady < changed}
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No post-discharge leakage (R4 clean) — the discharge medication-reconciliation status, including dose Up/Down decisions, is set at/by discharge. No R5 interaction. Caveat: do not describe the relationship as strictly monotonic in dose direction (Down > Up); and ensure the Up/Steady/Down encoding is applied consistently across all med columns so the shared aggregate count is coherent.
alternatives_considered: One-hot all four levels (chosen)
Ordinal not-on < Steady < changed
Binary insulin_changed flag (add)
Collapse to on/off (rejected: discards the dose-change signal)

columns.semantic_meaning: Fixed-dose combination of a sulfonylurea (glyburide) and metformin. Encodes prescription/dose status (No/Steady/Up/Down) during the encounter. Known at discharge, valid feature.
columns.value_interpretation: Near-constant per facts: No = 101060 (99.306%, pr 0.11161), Steady = 692 (0.680%, pr 0.11127), Up = 8 (0.008%, pr 0.0), Down = 6 (0.006%, pr 0.16667). 'Steady' (n = 692) is the best-populated non-No level of any combination drug in this family, but is still < 0.7% of rows, and its positive rate sits exactly at base rate. Up/Down (n = 8/6) are noise.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0. Status explicitly coded; no NaN. 'No' = not prescribed, informative, not missing. Not in co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: Effectively none. target_lift = 1.0; No pr = 0.11161 and Steady pr = 0.11127 are both indistinguishable from the 0.1116 base rate. Up (0.0, n = 8) and Down (0.16667, n = 6) are noise. No class separation — notably, even at the best-populated non-No level (Steady, n = 692) there is no lift.
columns.recommended_strategy: Drop as a standalone one-hot (near-zero variance: 99.306% 'No', and even the densest non-No level 'Steady' shows zero lift). If any combination-drug information is retained, do it via a shared binary 'on_combination_oral_antidiabetic' flag aggregated across all combo drugs, plus folding the 14 Up/Down rows into the shared 'n_meds_changed' count — not a per-drug one-hot.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation (0% missing). Drop justified by variance AND by the fact that Steady's positive rate equals the base rate (no signal even where the level is best-populated).
columns.modeling_transform: drop standalone; optionally fold into a shared combo-drug on/off flag and the shared meds-changed count
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No leakage (R4 clean), no R5 interaction. Caveat: Steady at n = 692 looks 'real' but its positive rate equals base rate — do not be fooled into keeping it for a non-existent signal.
alternatives_considered: One-hot four levels
Binary on/off
Fold into shared combo-drug flag + meds-changed count
Drop (chosen)

columns.semantic_meaning: Fixed-dose combination of a sulfonylurea (glipizide) and metformin. Encodes prescription/dose status (No/Steady) during the encounter. Known at discharge, valid feature.
columns.value_interpretation: Effectively constant per facts: No = 101753 (99.987%, pr 0.1116), Steady = 13 (0.013%, pr 0.07692). Only 13 rows record this drug; no Up/Down levels. Near-zero variance.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0. Explicitly coded status; no NaN. 'No' = not prescribed, informative, not missing. Not in co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: None. target_lift = 1.0; No pr = 0.1116 (= base rate); Steady pr = 0.07692 on n = 13 is noise. No discriminative power.
columns.recommended_strategy: Drop (near-zero variance: 99.987% 'No', only 13 non-No rows). 'Steady' is not a dose change, so it cannot feed a meds-changed count; at most it could feed a shared 'n_meds_on' count, but at n = 13 that contribution is negligible. Remove the column.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation (0% missing). Drop justified by near-constant variance and absence of any fold-level signal at n = 13.
columns.modeling_transform: drop
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No leakage (R4 clean), no R5 interaction. Caveat: rare-level positive rate is noise.
alternatives_considered: Binary flag
Drop (chosen)

columns.semantic_meaning: Fixed-dose combination of a sulfonylurea (glimepiride) and a thiazolidinedione (pioglitazone). Encodes prescription/dose status (No/Steady) during the encounter. Known at discharge, valid feature.
columns.value_interpretation: VIRTUALLY CONSTANT per facts: No = 101765 (99.999%, pr 0.1116), Steady = 1 (0.001%, pr 0.0). A single encounter in the whole dataset records this drug — a constant for all practical purposes.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0; only 1 non-No row. No NaN; 'No' is the coded 'not prescribed', not a disguised missing. Not in co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: None. target_lift = 1.0; 99.999% 'No' at base rate; the single 'Steady' row (pr 0.0) is meaningless. No separation possible.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP. With exactly 1 non-No row it is statistically a constant and will be removed by any VarianceThreshold guard. A one-hot 'Steady' here would be a single-1 dummy that can only memorize one training row — actively harmful, especially under grouped CV. Remove entirely.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation (0% missing). Drop justified: a single non-baseline observation is indistinguishable from a constant and creates an overfitting dummy.
columns.modeling_transform: drop
columns.leakage_or_caveat: No leakage (R4 clean), no R5 interaction. Caveat: a single-1 dummy is a textbook overfitting trap — under grouped CV it lands entirely in one fold.
alternatives_considered: Binary flag (rejected: single-1 dummy)
Drop (chosen)

columns.semantic_meaning: Fixed-dose combination of metformin and a thiazolidinedione (rosiglitazone). Encodes prescription/dose status (No/Steady) during the encounter. Known at discharge, valid feature.
columns.value_interpretation: VIRTUALLY CONSTANT per facts: No = 101764 (99.998%, pr 0.1116), Steady = 2 (0.002%, pr 0.0). Only 2 encounters record this drug. Effectively a constant.
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0; 2 non-No rows. No NaN; 'No' is coded 'not prescribed', not a disguised missing. Not in co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: None. target_lift = 1.0; 99.998% 'No' at base rate; the 2 'Steady' rows (pr 0.0) are meaningless. No separation.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP. With 2 non-No rows it is effectively constant and caught by a VarianceThreshold guard. A one-hot here would be a 2-row dummy with no generalizable signal. Remove entirely.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation (0% missing). Drop justified by near-zero variance (2 of 101766 non-baseline) and the overfitting risk of an almost-singleton dummy.

Drop (chosen)

columns.semantic_meaning: Fixed-dose combination of metformin and a thiazolidinedione (pioglitazone). Encodes prescription/dose status (No/Steady) during the encounter. Known at discharge, valid feature.
columns.value_interpretation: VIRTUALLY CONSTANT per facts: No = 101765 (99.999%, pr 0.1116), Steady = 1 (0.001%, pr 0.0). A single encounter records this drug — a constant for all practical purposes (mirrors glimepiride-pioglitazone).
columns.missing_mechanism_rationale: n_missing = 0; exactly 1 non-No row. No NaN; 'No' is coded 'not prescribed', not a disguised missing. Not in co_missing list. MCAR/MAR/MNAR not applicable.
columns.target_relationship: None. target_lift = 1.0; 99.999% 'No' at base rate; the single 'Steady' row (pr 0.0) is meaningless. No separation possible.
columns.recommended_strategy: DROP. Exactly 1 non-No row makes it a de facto constant; a one-hot 'Steady' dummy would memorize a single training row. Removed by any VarianceThreshold guard. Remove entirely.
columns.imputation_rationale: No imputation (0% missing). Drop justified: a single non-baseline observation is a constant in practice and an overfitting dummy.
